title: History, Objects, References, and Forensic Inspection · History Brief: Object Identity
summary: Explain when and why a behavior changed using graph, path, object, author, and content evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git object identity increment in a history-forensics evidence packet. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Relate blobs, trees, commits, tags, object IDs, content identity, and repository reachability.
Query commit graph topology, decorations, authorship, dates, messages, and changed paths with an intentional log format.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Object Identity
steps.instruction:
theory case — History has commits a11ce00 → b00b1e5 → c0ffee4; a file moved from notes.txt to docs/notes.txt with unchanged bytes. Explain graph, object identity, path evidence, and blame limits.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Object Identity contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for relate blobs, trees, commits, tags, object ids, content identity, and repository reachability.

content.text: History has commits a11ce00 → b00b1e5 → c0ffee4; a file moved from notes.txt to docs/notes.txt with unchanged bytes. Explain graph, object identity, path evidence, and blame limits.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Object Identity condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Pro Git, Second Edition — Controls cumulative conceptual sequence from setup through internals, collaboration, tooling, and distributed workflows.

hints: Name the Object Identity precondition or invariant that the theory case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: History has commits a11ce00 → b00b1e5 → c0ffee4; a file moved from notes.txt to docs/notes.txt with unchanged bytes. Explain graph, object identity, path evidence, and blame limits.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Renaming a file changes the stored blob bytes and therefore always creates a new blob.

options.text: Renaming a file changes the stored blob bytes and therefore always creates a new blob. This does not resolve predict object identity.

For predict object identity, apply Object Identity without its stated preconditions, omit the a history-forensics evidence packet trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Object Identity for a history-forensics evidence packet.
Relate blobs, trees, commits, tags, object IDs, content identity, and repository reachability. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.

steps.title: Bound Object Identity
steps.instruction:
theory case — History has commits a11ce00 → b00b1e5 → c0ffee5; a file moved from notes.txt to docs/notes.txt with unchanged bytes. Explain graph, object identity, path evidence, and blame limits. Competency probe: Relate blobs, trees, commits, tags, object IDs, content identity, and repository reachability.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Object Identity application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for relate blobs, trees, commits, tags, object ids, content identity, and repository reachability.

content.text: Relate blobs, trees, commits, tags, object IDs, content identity, and repository reachability. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology. Apply that move specifically to Object Identity while producing a history-forensics evidence packet.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: History has commits a11ce00 → b00b1e5 → c0ffee4; a file moved from notes.txt to docs/notes.txt with unchanged bytes. Explain graph, object identity, path evidence, and blame limits. Competency probe: Relate blobs, trees, commits, tags, object IDs, content identity, and repository reachability. A defensible solution applies Object Identity, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a history-forensics evidence packet.

content.code:
# Worked focus: git-object-identity — Relate blobs, trees, commits, tags, object IDs, content identity, and repository reachability.
git log --graph --decorate --oneline --all
git show --stat --summary HEAD
git cat-file -t HEAD^{tree}
git log --follow -- docs/notes.txt
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Renaming a file changes the stored blob bytes and therefore always creates a new blob. This fails the Object Identity contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

options.text: The Object Identity record says a history-forensics evidence packet looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not resolve inspect object identity evidence.

steps.title: Build Object Identity evidence
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-object-identity-hajxhe" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Object Identity into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: git-object-identity-hajxhe
# hypothesis: [replace with one falsifiable cause]
# command: choose one bounded simulator observation
# observation: [replace with the result that changes the next decision]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Object Identity structural check is only one gate. a history-forensics evidence packet must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

steps.title: Defend the Object Identity model
steps.instruction: Write the theory evidence note for Object Identity: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — History has commits a11ce00 → b00b1e5 → c0ffee5; a file moved from notes.txt to docs/notes.txt with unchanged bytes. Explain graph, object identity, path evidence, and blame limits. Competency probe: Relate blobs, trees, commits, tags, object IDs, content identity, and repository reachability.
steps.why: Retrieving Object Identity through the concrete a history-forensics evidence packet case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Relate blobs, trees, commits, tags, object IDs, content identity, and repository reachability.
